DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PETITION TO OBTAIN PERSONAL PROPERTY UNDER PROBATE CODE SECTIONS 850 AND 859, CALENDAR OF DEC-12-2019 - NO HEARING IS HELD. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONER HAS NOT STATED A CLAIM AGAINST CHICAGO TITLE UNDER PROBATE CODE SECTIONS 850 AND 859. PETITIONER, A NONPARTY TO THE ESCROW HANDLED BY CHICAGO TITLE, HAS PLEADED NO FACTS ON THE BASIS OF WHICH CHICAGO TITLE HAD ANY DUTY TO PETITIONER TO INVESTIGATE THE AUTHORITY OF THE PARTIES TO THE ESCROW OR THE COMMERCIAL REASONABLENESS OF THE TRANSACTION IN ESCROW. NOR HAS PETITIONER PLEADED ANY NON-CONCLUSORY FACTS ON THE BASIS OF WHICH CHICAGO TITLE COULD BE FOUND TO HAVE AIDED AND ABETTED OR CONSPIRED WITH THE PURPORTED SELLER. PETITIONER HAS HAD A PRIOR OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND TO ADDRESS THESE ISSUES AND HAS NOT INDICATED ANY OTHER FACTS HE MIGHT PLEAD. GIVEN THE LAW GOVERNING DUTIES OF ESCROW AGENTS, THERE IS NO REASON TO THINK ANOTHER OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND COULD CURE THE DEFECTS. -

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF RESPONDENTS KILBY STENKAMP AND VAGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO AMEND; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, CALENDAR OF MAR-19-2020, DENIED - THE MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 581(F)(2) IS DENIED. THE SECOND AMENDED PETITION WAS FILED PRIOR TO THE MOTION TO DISMISS. ALTHOUGH THE AMENDED PETITION WAS FILED WELL BEYOND THE TIME ALLOWED BY THE ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER AND ALTHOUGH ARGUABLY NEITHER GOOD CAUSE NOR DILIGENCE JUSTIFIES THE DELAY; THE SECOND AMENDED PETITION, HAVING BEEN FILED, IS NOW THE OPERATIVE PLEADING. IN SIMILAR CIRCUMSTANCES, GITMED V. GENERAL MOTORS, CORP. (1994) 26 CAL.APP.4TH 824, 827-28 (EMPHASIS DELETED), HELD "THAT THE FILING OF THE AMENDED [PETITION] PREVENTED THE TRIAL COURT FROM ENTERTAINING THE MOTION TO DISMISS. THE PROPER PROCEDURE WOULD HAVE BEEN FOR THE DEFENDANT TO BRING A MOTION TO STRIKE THE AMENDMENT BEFORE MOVING TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT." SEE ALSO, LEADER V. HEALTH INDUSTRIES OF AMERICA, INC. (2001) 89 CAL.APP.4TH 603, 612-614 (MOTION TO STRIKE AND MOTION TO DISCMISS BROUGHT TOGETHER); BROWN V. BROWN (1959 169 CAL.APP.2D 54.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Jul-11-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hussein Saffouri appeared by zoom for and with Floyd Trammell (executor/petitioner) by zoom.

The court informed Mr. Saffouri that probate does not recognize defaults and stated that if a petition under Probate Code 850 is legally/procedurally in order, and a party who has title to or possession of the property has not responded, the Court may grant the petition. Mr. Saffouri stated that there is no response to his petition. The parties also informed the court that Donald Trammell passed away on February 25, 2023, that this needs to be resolved so that distribution can go the second beneficiaries and that Donald does not have any children. The court informed counsel that he needs to file a petition to determine how distribution is to be done. The Amended 950 Petition is continued to September 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to amend his petition and provide notice. The court informed Mr. Saffouri that the petition needs to be supplemented with the specific assets that are claimed by the estate, with the amount of double damages that are being requesting, and with the amount of attorney fees and costs that the executor is requesting to be charged against the three remaining respondents. The court also informed Mr. Saffouri that an accounting for the first 2-years for the period 12/10/17 through 12/31/19 is needed and asked him to remove the request for special notice for the parties who have settled. The trial hearing is off calendar. Status on close of estate is continued to November 29, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

Petition For Correction Modification Or Withdrawal Of Order
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Brown and Hussein Saffouri appeared by zoom for and with Floyd Trammell by zoom.
Kendall Layne appeared by zoom for Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network.

Ms. Brown confirmed that the work product behind this order is her work. The court stated that this should have come before it as an 850 petition, that this was initially sought by ex parte and then this petition, and admonished Ms. Brown for the manner in which she communicated with the probate examiner. The court also stated that things need to happen under the correct statutes in probate, that it can't vacate this order, that it is not his order to vacate, that there is no basis for reconsidering this order, that this is a classic 850 petition so it will consider it on this petition and that she is getting a break largely directed from the examiner. In addition, the court pointed out that when Ms. Brown is talking about the examiner, she is talking about the court, and stated that her comment that's in her papers about "If not for this sua sponte order, the funds would be in the executor's hands." is unrealistic, that the funds were placed in a blocked account and raised the issue of all of the other funds that belonged the estate having disappeared. Ms. Brown argued that there is no party to file an 850 against, that there is no trustee and no trust to serve, that the will revoked the trust in 2015 three years before the decedent died and that she thinks it is only appropriate for the court to withdraw the order at this time. Ms. Brown also stated that the order was issued because the parties were engaged in a very long will contest, that a party was claiming ownership of the asset and that the court did not know at the time who was entitled to the asset. The court stated that if the order was valid when it was issued, it still stands, that the court does not modify or withdraw orders, that it follows the Code of Civil Procedures and that if there was a problem with the underlying order, they should have appealed, and that no one did. Further discussion was held regarding there being a trustee at the time when the order was issued, the issue of whether or not that person should have been served, Mr. Porchia having opened the blocked account and being the signatory of that account, Ms. Brown's client not having access to the funds, these funds being the only funds the executor will be holding, these funds not being used to pay any legal fees or executor's fees without a court order, deposits that will be made by people that were part of the settlement, bond having been waived by the will, the form of the order just releasing the funds, Mr. Layne not being required to make an appearance unless he has a stake in the petition, etc. The Petition for Correction, Modification or Withdrawal of Order is under submission. Ms. Brown is to monitor the court's electronic register of actions.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

The Court informed the Parties that the probate matters will be set for hearing, stated that the issues raised in the probate Examiner's notes with any of these petitions have not been addressed, and that if the issues are not cleared up by the next calling, the matters will go off calendar. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nader Fannyan, Esq., counsel for Donald Trammell and Clinton Killian, guardian ad litem for Donald Trammell, Petitioners; Mark Wilson, Esq., counsel for Kilby Stenkamp, Respondent; Scott Long, Esq., counsel for Caroline Ingeborn and Gustav Lindqvist, Respondents.

At the Court's invitation, Mr. Fannyan refers the Court to C.C.P. 437c(h), arguing that discovery is not yet complete and, reciting the time line history of the case, that circumstances have prevented Petitioners from pursuing discovery in a timely manner. Mr. Fannyan also notes that due to the number of demurrers, no other parties have yet appeared in the case and as such, he has not been able to commence discovery against those parties. The Court notes a want of prosecution with respect to discovery as to these Respondents, citing the untimeliness of Petitioners' Motion to Compel. In response to the Court's inquiry, Mr. Fannyan contends that discovery as to the other parties will disclose collusion between sellers [and/or their agents] and Respondents/moving parties herein [and/or their agents] that would give rise to questions of fact as to whether Respondents herein were bona fide purchasers for value.

At the Court's invitation, Mr. Long contends that Petitioners' have presented no evidence, either by declaration or otherwise, that supports their C.C.P. 437c(h) proposition and no evidentiary showing to support their contention that discovery as to the other parties might lead to evidence of collusion. Mr. Fannyan refers the Court to Petitioners' Points and Authorities opposing the Motion for Summary Judgment which, while not evidentiary, nonetheless gives rise to the issue of incomplete discovery and possible questions of fact.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Nuque; Staff Attorney: Elif Sonmez-Kirsch. (D306)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the tentative ruling on Respondents' Caroline Ingeborn and Notice of Motion And Motion for Summary Judgment or, Alternatively, Motion for Summary Adjudication is adopted as follows:

Denied without prejudice to renewal or subsequent motion. Subsequent to this motion being filed on the basis of an affirmative defense to the First Amended Petition, Petitioner filed his Second Amended Petition, which--at least unless struck or dismissed--stands as the operative pleading. "[O]nce an amended complaint is filed, it is error to grant summary adjudication on a cause of action contained in a previous complaint." State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-31 (reversing grant of summary judgment on statute of limitations affirmative defense). "After a cause of action is amended, the court may rule in favor of the defendant if, upon subsequent motion, or perhaps renewal of the earlier motion if appropriately framed, it is shown . . . there are no triable material issues of fact which would permit recovery on that theory." (Id. emphasis deleted.) Even assuming the bona fide purchaser affirmative defense remains applicable to the amended Probate Code section 850 cause of action, the Court cannot reach the issue on this motion given the current procedural posture of the case. Nothing in Wang v. Nibbelink (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 1 affects or questions the holding of State Compensation Ins. Fund that a motion for summary judgment cannot be granted as to a superceded complaint, even though the motion seeks judgment on the basis of an affirmative defense arguably still applicable after the amendment.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda.

Motion To Expunge Petitioner'S Notice Of Pendency Of Action (Lis Penden)
Off Calendar May-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott D. Long, Esq. for Caroline Ingeborn and Gustav Lindqvist (Respondents/Moving Parties).
Jennifer Brown, Esq. for and with Gennette Trammell and Floyd Trammell (Petitioners).
Nadar Fannyan, Esq. for Clinton Killian (Guardian ad Litem).
Jennifer Supman, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Vanguard Properties and Kilby Stencamp (Respondents).
Tanner D. Brink, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Chicago Title Company (Respondent).

The matter is called. The Court states that only the lis pendens is remaining on calendar today and that the demurrers were continued to be heard with the 850 petition. (A joinder to the lis pendens by Respondents Vanguard Properties and Kilby Stencamp is also on calendar.)

Argument on Respondent's Caroline Ingeborn and Gustav Lindqvist's Motion to Expunge Petitioner's Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens) is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the matter is submitted by counsel. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Respondents Gustav Lindqvist's and Caroline Ingeborn's Motion to Expunge Petitioner's Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens) is granted. The April 30, 2018 lis pendens must be expunged. Petitioner has not met his statutory burden under CCP 405.31 to show that that December 21, 2017 petition to probate decedent's 2015 Will contains a "real property claim." (CCP 405.4, 405.20). A petition to probate a will is not a "claim" by which a petitioner is "prosecuting another for the declaration, enforcement, or protection of a right." (CCP 22). At most, if successful, Petitioner would gain standing to marshal and pursue property for the estate. The petitioner's appointment would not resolve any property rights the estate might claim or resolve any dispute as to the subject property. Further, the Court is required to expunge the lis pendens under CCP 405.32 because Petitioner has not met his statutory burden to establish "by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of the real property claim." The case Petitioner cites to argue that the Motion should be denied because he recorded the lis pendens in good faith and for a proper purpose has been superseded by statute; the statutes the case relies on have been repealed, and do not reflect the current statutory showing necessary to defeat a motion to expunge. Petitioner included no evidence whatsoever to demonstrate he would prevail on his petition to be appointed personal representative, let alone any evidence to show that his preferred version of the competing testamentary documents would be shown valid.
In light of the Court's conclusions, it is unnecessary to determine whether the lis pendens would also fail on the basis of notice. Ms. Brown reviewed the order in open court. The order is signed in open court.

The Court stated that it makes no ruling with regard to selling the property (etc.) and that all the Court is doing is following the rules with regard to the lis pendens action. Mr. Long addressed the continuance of the demurrers and the Court addressed the current posture of the litigation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, Tel. 925.384.0004. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Gennette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Jennifer Brown for and with Floyd Trammell (Petitioner).
Clinton Killian Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell (Sole Beneficiary).
Lindsey Munyer appeared by telephone for and with Lawrence Porchia (Successor Trustee present in Court).

Mr. Killian stated that two parties reached an agreement regarding who shall serve as the Executor and the Trustee, but there are substantive issues regarding sale of the property, that delay caused the estate to lose $500,000.00 in value, that there are over $500,000.00 in disbursements and payments that were paid by the Trustee to the detriment of the Beneficiary and that he has prepared an 850 Petition to recover $1,000,000.00. Ms. Munyer stated that she is hesitant to speak about the settlement agreement, that it is confidential and that it has not been memorialized. The Court stated that all it has before it is the competing petitions for probate and that it does not prevent the parties from asserting any claims. Ms. Munyer stated that she believes the competing petitions are settled, that the Guardian Ad Litem will not sign off on the agreement, that he was not part of mediation, that they do not have global settlement and requested that the Court set a mandatory settlement conference. Mr. Killian stated that the Guardian Ad Litem is neutral as to who serves, that the problem was when they found out that the property was sold without any notice and that they can not have any claims without having information. Mr. Killian also stated that the accounting was filed several months late and that it is not prudent for his Client to waive what appears to be a loss of $1,000,000.00. Ms. Brown stated that they reached an agreement for both parties to serve as Co-Administrators, and that the issues between Mr. Porchia and her Client are no longer and have been resolved. Mr. Killian stated that he has no objection to the parties being Co-Administrators, suggested continuing the competing petitions and stated that he will file a petition for appointment of a Special Administrator. The Court urges the parties to resolve what is before the Court, encouraged Mr. Killian to participate in anyway he can in broader settlement and encouraged the parties to get Mr. Killian what he needs. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, the Petition for Order Reforming, or Alternately Modifying the Terms of the Trust, etc. and status of mediation are continued to April 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Continued To Feb-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Genette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Timothy Bartteau for and with Lawrence Porchia (Trustee).
Clinton Killian Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell.
Jennifer Brown for and with Floyd Trammell.

Floyd Trammell stated that he thought they had an understanding, but it was not in writing. Mr. Barteau stated that they had a long round of mediation, that there is some issue as to the agreement, that he has contacted the Mediator to see if they can go back around it and that he has not heard back from the Mediator. Mr. Killian suggested a 30-day continuance to see if the parties can resolve this and if not, set the matter for hearing. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and status on mediation are continued to February 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to submit a joint status statement no later than February 13, 2019. If the case is not resolved, the parties are to meet and confer as to what the actual issues are, how the matter is going to go forward and clearing up any procedural issues.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Granted Per Written Order

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Continued To Oct-03-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 202

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Brown for and with (petitioner) Floyd Trammell. Timothy Barteau for and with (objector) Lawrence Porchia. Clinton Killion, Guardian ad Litem for Donald Trammell.

The Court stated that there are competing petitions with various procedural issues, another petition set for November 19, 2018, that this is generating a lot of ill will when the parties agree on far more than they disagree on, that everyone agrees that the estate needs to be used for the benefit of Donald, that the 2004 version is not satisfactory to do so, that it is a priority to preserve Donald's benefits, that it is not sure why the parties cannot work things out, that it is eating up enormous amounts of time, money and creating ill will, that the Court can offer Pro Bono Mediation or suggest a 3rd party fiduciary. Ms. Brown stated that they are willing to participate in mediation but that there is still the issue of standing and that they have submitted objections. The Court stated that there is nothing in front of it to show lack of standing, that it is not productive and that the parties will spend time and money fighting the 2015 documents. Both counsel agree to Pro Bono Mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 9, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 14, 2018. The two petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, as well as the Petition for Order Reforming, etc. in case PTR-18-302129 (currently set for 11/19/18) are continued to and the Parties are to return on November 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Brown for Floyd Trammel (Petitioner).
Timothy Barteau for and with Lawrence Porchia.

Ms. Brown confirmed that the ex parte petition regarding disclosure of physician contact information is moot. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not it is appropriate for Mr. Killian to be appointed as Guardian Ad Litem and spoke of Mr. Killian's background as well as a need for the Guardian Ad Litem to have knowledge about special needs trusts. Mr. Barteau raised the issue of the petition needing to amended for probate of a lost will and requested an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Brown spoke of the difficulty in getting to this point and delay. The ex parte petition for appointment of Guardian Ad Litem is under submission. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-09-2018 Continued To Jun-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Brown for and with Floyd Trammell (Petitioner)
Timothy Barteau for and with Lawrence Portia (Objector)
Alexis Cook of Adult Protective Services

Mr. Barteau stated that he will be filing a substitution of attorney into this case, will be requesting medical documents or at least medical contact information, knows of at least ARC, and will be requesting an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Brown stated that they will need documents, for Donald Trammell to be examined, and Ms. Cook is willing to be Guardian Ad Litem, evaluation of Donald Trammell can be done in the next few weeks. The Court noted the conflict between the parties, hope they will re-direct, and extend cooperation to deal with decedent's estate; will exchange information, or come up with a plan acceptable to everybody.
The Petition for Probate is continued to June 20, 2018 at 9:00a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Continued To May-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Brown for Floyd Trammell (Petitioner)
Lawrence Porchia (in Propria Persona)

The Court noted there has been no response to the Probate Examiner's notes, the matter is not in a position for it to act on, there are a couple of ex parte requests, one is missing notice, and it is not clear if it is a proper ex parte petition. Ms. Brown stated that she prepared an amended petition within one week of receiving the Probate Examiner's notes, the Probate Examiner raised the issue of whether a Guardian Ad Litem should be appointed for the disabled beneficiary, that the beneficiary now lives with his sister and he was directed not to communicate with anyone regarding the petition. Ms. Brown also stated that she filed an ex parte request to compel release of Donald Trammell's medical information, and she asked for a Court Investigator to be appointed to interview Mr. Trammell. The Court noted that this is not within the scope of the Court Investigator's duties. Mr. Porchia disagreed with Ms. Brown's comments regarding communication, asked for the Probate Petition to be dismissed, and stated that Counsel and the Administrator were taking advantage of Mrs. Trammell. Ms. Brown stated she does not believe they will get cooperation, and she also reached ARC and GGRC. The Court noted the family has information and encourages them to provide it to Ms. Brown. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to May 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
